content: Never commit Python cache files (__pycache__) to version control — add __pycache__/ to .gitignore and remove these files from the repository to keep it clean
Always verify all imported modules and dependencies exist before referencing them — missing files like database.py, models.py, schemas.py cause critical import errors and application failures
Always complete test files without truncation — incomplete files with truncated functions cause syntax errors and break test runs
Never submit PRs with failing tests or truncated files — complete all test implementations and ensure they pass before requesting review
Always ensure database migration enum values match model definitions exactly — migration used 'PLANNING' but model used 'planning', causing runtime failures
Use proper foreign key relationships in tests instead of hardcoded IDs — hardcoded values like venue_id=1, concert_id=1 don't test actual relationships and will fail with real database constraints
Always implement CSRF protection and XSS escaping when using HTMX forms — use Flask-WTF CSRF tokens on data-modifying endpoints (hx-post, hx-delete) and escape user content with {{ content|e }}

Mock chained method calls require explicit return_value assignment at each level — use mock_db.query.return_value.filter.return_value.first.return_value instead of mock_db.query().filter().first.return_value
Never manually manage database sessions by calling next(get_db()) and db.close() — use FastAPI dependency injection or proper session context management instead of unsafe manual session handling
Never mix framework-specific syntax between different frameworks — using Flask url_for() in FastAPI templates causes incompatibility errors
Never use CORS allow_origins=['*'] in production code — make CORS configuration environment-dependent or use specific origins for security
Test public interface methods rather than private methods — avoid testing private methods like _validate_venue_data() directly and instead test the public methods that use them
Remove unused imports to maintain clean code — importing EmailStr and validator from pydantic when validation is implemented as standalone methods violates code conventions
Make hardcoded business values configurable through constants or environment variables — avoid magic numbers like ticket prices and rates embedded in code
Use request body with proper schema instead of query parameters for POST endpoints — POST endpoint should use proper schema in request body rather than query parameters
Validate data integrity constraints at the model level — enforce business rules like preventing negative durations and implementing proper field validation
Never remove essential dependencies that are still in use — pytest, pydantic, and alembic were removed from requirements but tests still import pytest and models need pydantic for schemas
Use modern SQLAlchemy 2.0 DeclarativeBase instead of deprecated declarative_base() — import from sqlalchemy.orm and create class Base(DeclarativeBase)
Use FastAPI lifespan context manager instead of deprecated @app.on_event('startup') for FastAPI 0.104.1+ — the on_event decorators are being phased out

Never change frameworks without implementing the complete backend infrastructure — switching from FastAPI to Flask requires implementing all routes, models, and app setup before templates can function
Always implement pagination on collection GET endpoints — missing limit/offset parameters on GET /concerts/ endpoint violates architecture decisions and causes performance issues with large datasets
Implement foundational layers before dependent components — create models, services, and backend endpoints before writing tests or frontend code that depends on them

Always include referenced template files in PRs when implementing routes — referencing add_concert.html, view_setlist.html, add_venue.html, and venues.html templates without including them breaks the application
API endpoints should return JSON data following REST conventions — returning raw HTML from API endpoints violates standards and makes testing/maintenance difficult
Define shared Base classes in a central module like models/base.py rather than locally with declarative_base() — local definitions cause import issues when other models need the same Base
Integrate validation methods into models or remove them — defining validate_email() and validate_capacity() methods without connecting them to SQLAlchemy using @validates decorator creates dead code
Quality gate 'coverage' failed — {"percent": 0.0, "threshold": 70, "status": "fail"}
Quality gate 'coverage' failed — {"percent": 0.0, "threshold": 70, "status": "fail"}
Never submit truncated or incomplete code files — file truncated at line 306 with incomplete method will cause syntax errors and break the application
Use modern SQLAlchemy 2.0 syntax with .where() instead of deprecated .filter() on count() results — SQLAlchemy 1.x syntax is incompatible with modern versions
Make cache TTL and other business values configurable through environment variables — avoid hardcoded values like 300 seconds following project conventions
Never submit incomplete test files with truncated methods — test file truncated at line 450 with incomplete `test_get_venue_not_found` method causing syntax errors
Implement comprehensive test coverage for all CRUD operations — missing test classes for POST, PUT, and DELETE endpoints leaves create, update, and delete operations untested
Add business rule validation beyond basic schema validation — missing input validation for duplicate venues, capacity limits, and other business constraints on venue creation
Always include httpx dependency when using FastAPI TestClient — missing httpx dependency will cause import failures in tests that use TestClient
Use proper SQLAlchemy 2.0 session patterns in tests with contextlib.contextmanager — avoid manual session management with try/except StopIteration handling
Make database URLs configurable via environment variables — avoid hardcoded database connection strings for different environments (dev/test/prod)
Avoid circular imports by placing shared base classes in separate modules — database.py importing Base from models.py while models.py needs database components creates circular dependency
Always call database initialization functions or document their usage — create_tables() function was defined but never called, leaving database tables uncreated
Use SQLAlchemy Enum type for proper database constraints — storing enum values as strings without database-level enum constraints reduces data integrity
Always complete test files without truncation — test file ending with 'de' on line 275 causes syntax errors and violates known pitfall
Never remove dependencies without justification — aiosqlite and httpx dependencies were removed without verification they are truly unused
Always include comprehensive edge case testing — missing tests for zero ticket prices, large numbers, timezone handling, and schema validation
Never leave test files with incomplete function definitions — truncated test files cause syntax errors and break the test suite
Always verify all imported modules exist before deployment — missing imports for models and routers cause import errors at runtime
Always implement comprehensive test coverage including dedicated unit tests for service layer business logic — endpoint tests alone are insufficient

Never submit PRs with incomplete or truncated files — the diff was truncated at line 306 with an incomplete method `_calculate_progress_percentage`, which violates code submission requirements and causes syntax errors
Make cache configuration values configurable through constants or environment variables — avoid hardcoding TTL values like 300 seconds directly in the code
Implement proper error handling for external dependencies — service methods should handle database connection failures and query timeouts gracefully with appropriate fallback responses

Never create circular imports between modules — database.py importing from models.py while models.py needs to import from database.py creates circular dependency. Move shared classes like Base to a separate module or to the importing module

Use SQLAlchemy's Enum type for enum storage instead of String — storing enum as String(20) and comparing string values will fail. Use `from sqlalchemy import Enum` and `mapped_column(Enum(EnumClass))`
Ensure enum values match expected test outputs — test expects 'planning' but TourStatus.PLANNING.value is 'PLANNING'. Maintain consistency between enum values and test expectations
Never submit PRs with truncated or incomplete files — test file ended with incomplete function 'de' causing syntax errors
Never remove dependencies without verification they aren't used elsewhere — removing aiosqlite and httpx could break database connectivity or HTTP client functionality
Always validate data integrity constraints at the model level — add model validators to enforce business rules like ensuring end_date is after start_date
Always create or reference existing modules before importing — importing database.py and models.py modules that don't exist causes import failures
Use Pydantic v2 model_dump() method instead of deprecated dict() method — dict() doesn't exist in Pydantic v2 and causes runtime errors
Use in-memory or shared test databases with proper cleanup instead of separate database files — creating separate test database files (test_concerts.db, test_tours.db) causes test isolation issues
Always verify module structure and imports before using relative imports — import from ..models will fail if models/__init__.py doesn't exist or doesn't export the required classes
Always add input validation for API endpoints that accept data — create_venue and update_venue accept raw dict without validating required fields or data types, which could cause database errors
Always implement error handling for database operations — db.commit() could fail due to constraint violations or connection issues, causing unhandled exceptions
Never import routers or modules that don't exist in the codebase — importing non-existent concerts and venues routers causes import errors and application startup failures
Always complete function implementations in test files — truncated test functions cause syntax errors and break test runs
Ensure all model imports reference existing modules — importing from non-existent model modules like ..models.concert and ..models.venue breaks the application
Never truncate test files in the middle of methods — test file truncated at line 192 will cause syntax errors and break test runs
Use environment variables for CORS origins in all environments — avoid hardcoded origins even in development configuration
Use modern Pydantic methods instead of deprecated ones — replace .dict() with .model_dump() for future compatibility

Avoid circular imports by moving shared Base classes to a separate module — database.py imports from models.py but models.py will likely need to import from database.py, create a separate base.py file instead
All tests must pass before submitting PR — PR description stated 'Some tests failing — needs review' but didn't specify which tests or why, identify and fix all failing tests before merge
Use SQLAlchemy's native Enum type instead of String for enum columns — String(20) for TourStatus enum could cause issues if enum values change length, prefer SQLAlchemy Enum type for type safety
Never submit PRs with truncated or incomplete files — test file ended with incomplete 'de' and missing test implementations, which violates basic quality standards
Never remove dependencies without explanation or verification they're unused — aiosqlite and httpx were removed without justification and may be required for database operations and testing
Always implement business logic validation in data models — missing validation that end_date is after start_date for tours and that concert dates fall within tour date ranges
Always include all referenced modules and dependencies in PRs — missing database.py and models.py caused ImportError and application startup failure
Use Pydantic v2 syntax model_dump() instead of deprecated dict() method — reviewer flagged concert.dict() and tour.dict() as deprecated
Always verify test files are complete and syntactically valid — truncated test_create_tour function caused syntax errors breaking test runs
Never remove existing schema imports without verification they aren't used elsewhere — completely removing Tour and Concert schema imports can break dependencies in other parts of the codebase
Always implement proper format validation for structured data fields — zip_code validator should use regex pattern like '^\d{5}(-\d{4})?$' instead of just checking for digits and hyphens
Always validate URL format when accepting URL inputs — use pydantic's HttpUrl type or custom validator for website fields instead of accepting any string
Implement foundational models before dependent components — import statements for models will fail if the models module/package doesn't exist in the repository yet
Whitelist allowed fields or use proper schema for update operations — using hasattr() check without validating against dangerous fields like 'id' creates security risks
Make hardcoded business values configurable through constants — magic number 100 for default limit should be made configurable following project convention
Never submit incomplete or truncated files that break syntax — test file truncated at line 208 with incomplete function causes test run failures
Use restrictive CORS defaults instead of permissive environment-controlled origins — avoid allowing any origin through environment manipulation, use explicit origins list
Never reference non-existent imports or dependencies in code — importing routers for 'concerts' and 'venues' that don't exist yet causes import errors
Never submit PRs with truncated or incomplete code files — code truncated at line 202 causing syntax errors and making files unusable
Make hardcoded configuration values like cache TTL configurable through constants or environment variables — avoid magic numbers like hardcoded 300 seconds TTL
Never submit PRs with failing tests without including the test files for review — violates convention of addressing test failures before submission
Always complete all test method implementations before submitting — truncated test files with incomplete methods cause syntax errors and break test runs
Use Pydantic v2 syntax with `from_attributes = True` and `.model_dump()` instead of deprecated `orm_mode = True` and `.dict()` methods
Verify all imported modules exist before referencing them in code — importing non-existent modules like `database.py` and `models.py` causes import errors
Never enable SQL logging in production code — using echo=True in SQLAlchemy engine will log all SQL statements to console and should be configurable via environment variables
Resolve import dependencies before database engine creation — models.py imports from Base class that needs to be defined before the engine is created to avoid circular dependencies
Use consistent enum value formats in database models — enum defines uppercase values but defaults may store lowercase due to string representation mismatches
Always include all required dependencies in PRs — importing database.py and models.py modules that don't exist in the codebase causes critical import failures that prevent application startup
Never remove dependencies without verifying they are unused — removing aiosqlite and pytest-asyncio dependencies may break async database operations and testing functionality
Make CORS configuration environment-dependent rather than hardcoded — allowing only localhost:3000 is too restrictive and should be configurable based on deployment environment
Never remove essential dependencies without confirming they are truly unused — removing uvicorn and pydantic that are likely needed for FastAPI application breaks functionality
Always maintain backward compatibility when modifying imports — changing Base import source and removing model imports can break existing code dependencies
Use established Base classes from central modules instead of defining new ones — creating new Base class instead of importing from existing database module causes table conflicts
Never remove existing schema imports when adding new ones — removing TourCreate, TourUpdate and other tour/concert schemas breaks existing functionality that depends on them
Implement comprehensive input validation on user data fields — phone numbers should have format validation and zip codes should allow spaces for US format '12345 6789'
Remove redundant import-only test classes — testing imports separately is unnecessary when functional tests already verify schema functionality

Use absolute imports or verify package structure instead of relative imports — `from ..models import Venue` will cause ImportError if parent package structure doesn't exist, should use `from src.models import Venue` or proper relative imports
Always add input validation in service layer methods — validate required fields and data types in create_venue() and update_venue() before database operations to prevent SQLAlchemy errors
Use OR logic for search queries across multiple fields — search_query should use OR with name/address/description fields rather than AND logic when filtering venues
Never reference non-existent modules or files in imports — importing concerts and venues routers that don't exist will cause import errors and application startup failures
Always ensure test files are complete and syntactically valid — truncated test functions will cause syntax errors and test failures
Remove unused imports to maintain clean code — importing Dict, Any, Optional when not used violates code cleanliness standards
Never submit incomplete code files that are truncated mid-function — file truncated at line 306 in the middle of _calculate_progress_percentage method will cause syntax errors
Always verify imported dependencies exist and match expected interfaces before submission — code imports from .models, .schemas, and .cache but dependencies need verification
Implement thread-safe caching mechanisms or document single-threaded usage requirements — InMemoryCache is not thread-safe and could cause data corruption in concurrent environments
Never submit incomplete test files with truncated methods — test file truncated at line 450 with incomplete `test_get_venue_not_found` method causing syntax errors and preventing tests from running
Always handle missing environment variables gracefully in configuration — CORS configuration should default to restrictive settings if ENVIRONMENT variable is unset
Use Pydantic v2 syntax instead of deprecated methods — replace `.dict()` with `.model_dump()` and `orm_mode = True` with `from_attributes = True` for future compatibility
Avoid circular imports by placing shared Base classes in separate modules — database.py importing from models.py while models.py needs database components creates circular dependency
Never remove dependencies without justification — alembic, aiosqlite, pytest-asyncio, httpx were removed without explanation but may be needed for migrations and testing
Ensure enum values match database constraints — TourStatus enum uses 'planning' but should be consistent with actual stored/retrieved database values
Always complete test files without truncation — test file truncated at line 259 with incomplete function will cause syntax errors
Never remove dependencies without verification — removing aiosqlite and httpx dependencies without explanation could break existing functionality that depends on these packages
Always validate that update requests contain at least one field — TourUpdate and ConcertUpdate schemas don't prevent empty update requests
Always include all dependency files in the PR — importing database.py and models.py files that don't exist in the PR will cause the application to fail to start
Never submit truncated code files — incomplete functions like test_create_tour cause syntax errors that break test execution
Implement complete CRUD operations when PR title specifies full functionality — missing UPDATE and DELETE operations for complete CRUD as promised
Quality gate 'coverage' failed — {"percent": 0.0, "threshold": 70, "status": "fail"}
